nodes.label: AI as communication polishing tool
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to help me formulate my thoughts, word things better, make my tone more professional

nodes.label: AI as thinking partner for complex explanations
nodes.grounding_span: if I don't know exactly what I want to say (it's a more difficult explanation/question) and I need to organize my thoughts

nodes.label: AI alignment with company policies and specific workflows
nodes.label_negative: AI deviation from company policies and specific workflows
nodes.grounding_span: It doesn't always take into account company policies, and so I often have to fix things

nodes.label: AI flexibility in adapting to patient-specific changes
nodes.label_negative: AI rigidity in assuming diagnoses and resisting changes
nodes.grounding_span: It often assumes diagnoses and makes a big deal if I try to change the recommended exercises

nodes.label: AI integration with existing nuanced clinical practices
nodes.label_negative: AI disruption of established nuanced clinical practices
nodes.grounding_span: the AI created its own detailed description of the exercise (of exactly what the video showed) and I now have to go in and create a separate note that basically says 'ignore everything that is written and do this:'

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner throughout task
nodes.grounding_span: I'm more likely to collaborate with it throughout a task

nodes.label: AI as automatic response drafter with human oversight
nodes.label_negative: Manual response drafting without AI assistance
nodes.grounding_span: I'd love for it to get to a point where it drafts an automatic response to emails/text/app chats based on prior history and understanding of company policies

nodes.label: Efficient patient communication
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to help me formulate my thoughts, word things better, make my tone more professional

nodes.label: Workflow efficiency and reduced workload
nodes.grounding_span: it would make things super easy

nodes.label: Professional autonomy and control
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle it myself since it's less overall work for me

nodes.label: Patient-centered care and relationship
nodes.grounding_span: AI can't socialize/create a relationship with Dr. Snow the way I do

nodes.label: Strong preference for AI in communication tasks
nodes.grounding_span: When it comes to writing/patient communication I 100% prefer AI to handle it

nodes.label: Frustration with AI in program creation due to policy misalignment
nodes.grounding_span: It doesn't always take into account company policies, and so I often have to fix things

nodes.label: Frustration with AI rigidity in program creation
nodes.grounding_span: It often assumes diagnoses and makes a big deal if I try to change the recommended exercises

nodes.label: Satisfaction with collaborative AI interaction
nodes.grounding_span: I'm more likely to collaborate with it throughout a task

nodes.label: Optimistic desire for AI to handle automatic drafting
nodes.grounding_span: I'd love for it to get to a point where it drafts an automatic response

nodes.label: Task triaging and selective AI engagement
nodes.grounding_span: Any that are simple to answer and that I don't need help with, I will go ahead and complete

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through prompt tweaking
nodes.grounding_span: Usually this type of prompt will take a few tweaks after the initial prompt
